Early-career fundraising and partnerships professional supporting donor pipeline development and multi-country resource mobilization across seven Asia-Pacific markets at Habitat for Humanity International. Magna Cum Laude graduate in International Studies, Xavier University, 2026.

Live Across The Region

Seven Asia-Pacific Markets, One Pipeline

Coordinating donor relationships and funding opportunities across the Philippines, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Nepal, Cambodia, Vietnam, and Fiji.

About Me

Professional Summary

I grew up in a small house in Lugait, Misamis Oriental. My father farms the land there. My mother works overseas so our family could keep that house. Long before I had a title in institutional fundraising, I understood what it costs a family to hold onto stability, and what it means when someone far away makes a decision that changes that.

That is the lens I carry into this work. At Habitat for Humanity International's Asia Pacific office, I manage funding pipelines across seven countries: the Philippines, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Nepal, Cambodia, Vietnam, and Fiji. Every row in a donor tracker is a grant proposal, a relationship, and a family waiting on a decision made in a room far from where they live.

I studied International Studies at Xavier University, Ateneo de Cagayan, graduating Magna Cum Laude in 2026. The multilateral frameworks I read about in class, the EU, the World Bank, the ADB, became live documents the moment I started tracking real Go/No-Go decisions against them.

Professional Experience

Global Foundations, Organizations & Institutions (GFOI) Intern
Habitat for Humanity International – Asia Pacific Office
Dec 2025 – Present

Habitat for Humanity International works in nearly 70 countries, helping families build and improve places to call home.

The work starts long before anyone sees a decision. Every week I scan Devex, Devaid, LinkedIn, AsianNGO, and Terra Viva for opportunities that might fit one of seven country programs, then walk each one through a documentation trail, After Action Reviews, RFP summaries, partnership prep, until it's ready for a Go or a No-Go.

The tool that holds this together is one I built myself: a live Partnership Mapping workbook and a Conversion Tracker, because the process before it couldn't show a National Office where its own pipeline stood at a glance. Multiply that across seven markets and a spreadsheet stops being a spreadsheet. It becomes the paper trail someone uses to decide whether a housing program moves forward.

5–10 opportunities vetted weekly 52 tracked across a 7-stage pipeline 7 National Offices, coordinated monthly
Corporate Relations & Communications Intern
European Chamber of Commerce of the Philippines – Northern Mindanao
Sep – Dec 2025

ECCP represents European business interests in the Philippines, connecting corporate members with government and industry partners.

Membership growth rarely comes from one campaign. It comes from following up on the outreach nobody else has time for. I rebuilt the onboarding sequence for prospective members and matched content to the platforms our audience actually used instead of the ones we defaulted to, then spent five events on the ground making sure the relationships built online held up in person.

20% membership growth, one quarter 35% lift in engagement 5+ chamber events coordinated
HR & Business Development Intern
WeSource Management Consultancy
Jul – Aug 2025

WeSource provides recruitment and business development consulting for companies across Northern Mindanao.

Recruiting is a pipeline problem before it's a people problem. Postings were living separately on Manatal, Indeed, and JobStreet with no shared view of where a candidate actually stood, so I rebuilt the process around a single tracking view and rewrote the employer-branding content tied to each campaign.

30% more applicants 20% faster time-to-fill

Projects & Achievements

Layalayag AI Kiosk
AmCham Philippines Business Leadership Program 2025
🏆 Champion · Business Plan Competition
Challenge

Fisherfolk communities across BARMM lack access to real-time weather data, fair pricing information, and sustainable catch-management tools, in a region where 43.9% of the population lives below the poverty line.

My Role
  • Led the pitch team designing an AI-driven kiosk concept for 500,000+ artisanal fisherfolk.
  • Grounded the concept in the realities of a farming family rather than the industries the program usually rewarded.
  • Built the business case and financial projections, then pitched to a panel of corporate sponsors.
Results

Placed 1st among the full BLP 2025 cohort, one of 30 finalist teams selected from 500+ applicants nationwide (top 6%), sponsored by JP Morgan, Citibank, Ford, BPI, Capital One, and Quezon Power.

8th Mindanao Intervarsity
Xavier Debate Circle
🏆 Extracurricular Project of the Year
Challenge

Organize the largest regional academic debate tournament in Mindanao while securing enough funding to guarantee a genuinely level playing field for schools that never had one.

My Role
  • Served as Tournament Director, directing every operational, logistical, and stakeholder requirement.
  • Generated ₱356,500 in sponsorships through SM City CDO Uptown and the CDO Vice Mayor's Office.
  • Built the stage specifically for debaters from underfunded schools across Mindanao and the Visayas.
Results

Hosted 150+ participants; recognized as Extracurricular Project of the Year at Xavier University, ahead of 20+ competing student organizations.

MarkProf · Dec 2024 – Mar 2025
Top 10 Graduate, Top 25 Marketing Management

One of 25 delegates chosen from 1,000+ applicants nationwide (top 1%), training alongside UnionBank, Unilever, Mondelez, Cebu Pacific, and P&G.

GCash Philippines · 2026
Top 10 Nationally, ImagNation Hackathon

Placed top 10 out of 1,000+ student participants, designing an automated funnel budget tracker for GCash's product ecosystem.

Xavier University · 2022 – 2026
Magna Cum Laude · CAS Outstanding Graduate

Philippine Sociological Society · 2026
Paper Presenter, International Conference

Presented original undergraduate research on the people-level impacts of ASEAN cyber governance frameworks over a ten-year observation window.

Peer-reviewed · SKHID (EAST), 8(2)

DOI: 10.21847/2411-3093.2026.825
